19.gl: enough (as noun or adverb), used chiefly with preposition in phrases
19.kj: able, according to, after (ability), among, as (oft as), (more than) enough, from, in, since, (much as is) sufficient(-ly), too much, very, when.

to prolong, i.e. (reflex) be deformed by excess of members
20.kj: stretch out self, (have any) superfluous thing.

21.kj: (come from) another (man, place), fanner, go away, (e-) strange(-r, thing, woman).

21.gl: to quiver (with any violent emotion, especially anger or fear)

22.kj: appointed time, ([phrase]) army, ([phrase]) battle, company, host, service, soldiers, waiting upon, war(-fare).

22.kj: ambassador, have in derision, interpreter, make a mock, mocker, scorn(-er, -ful), teacher.

22.gl: not (the qualified negation, used as a deprecative); once (Job 24:25) as a noun, nothing
22.kj: nay, neither, [phrase] never, no, nor, not, nothing (worth), rather than.

23.dv: a primitive root; probably to expand; but used only as a denominative from H241 (אֹזֶן);

23.gl: to prick up the ears, i.e. hearken

23.dv: or אֶמְרָה; feminine of H561 (אֵמֶר), and meaning the same

24.dv: from an unused root meaning to be hot;

24.kj: appear, break forth, draw (out), let go free, (en-) grave(-n), loose (self), (be, be set) open(-ing), put off, ungird, unstop, have vent.

24.gl: to sow; figuratively, to disseminate, plant, fructify
24.kj: bear, conceive seed, set with sow(-er), yield.

25.gl: the face (as the part that turns); used in a great variety of applications (literally and figuratively); also (with prepositional prefix) as a preposition (before, etc.)
25.kj: [phrase] accept, a-(be-) fore(-time), against, anger, [idiom] as (long as), at, [phrase] battle, [phrase] because (of), [phrase] beseech, countenance, edge, [phrase] employ, endure
25.dv: plural (but always as singular) of an unused noun פָּנֶה; from H6437 (פָּנָה));

25.kj: [idiom] any wise, appoint, bring, call (a name), care, cast in, change, charge, commit, consider, convey, determine, [phrase] disguise, dispose, do, get, give, heap up, hold, imput

25.gl: properly, a ring, i.e. (by analogy) a row (adverbially)

26.dv: or (2 Chronicles 26:15) יָרָא; a primitive root;

26.gl: to chastise, literally (with blows) or figuratively (with words); hence, to instruct
26.kj: bind, chasten, chastise, correct, instruct, punish, reform, reprove, sore, teach.

27.dv: or חָרֻץ; passive participle of H2782 (חָרַץ);

a scion, i.e. (literally) a stick (for punishing, writing, fighting, ruling, walking, etc.) or (figuratively) a clan

27.dv: from an unused root probably meaning to branch off;

27.dv: or דּוֹשׁ; or דִּישׁ; a primitive root;
from an unused root meaning to store up or preserve;

beat in pieces (small), bruise, make dust, (into) [idiom] powder, (be, very) small, stamp (small).

properly, to put in commotion; by implication, to disturb, drive, destroy
28.kj: break, consume, crush, destroy, discomfit, trouble, vex.

28.kj: break, tear, thresh, tread out (down), at grass (Jeremiah 50:11, by mistake for H1877 (דֶּשֶׁא)).
28.dv: or דּוֹשׁ; or דִּישׁ; a primitive root;

29.kj: accompanying, against, and, as ([idiom] long as), before, beside, by (reason of), for all, from (among, between), in, like, more than, of, (un-) to, with(-al).
